statements.__comment: Project `_ont_has_mfa` on AWSUser from the :MFA_DEVICE edge. AWS does not expose an MFA boolean on the IAM user (the credential report is not ingested), so we derive it from the presence of any AWSMfaDevice attached to the user.

Project `_ont_active = true` on AWSUser when there is positive activity evidence: the user has used the console at least once (passwordlastused_dt is set) OR owns at least one access key with status = 'Active'. Absence of evidence is reset to NULL (unknown) rather than `false`, because this sync does not ingest login profiles or credential reports and a NULL passwordlastused does not imply inactivity. The `MATCH ... SET = CASE` form (rather than `WHERE ... SET = true`) is required to clear stale `true` values on users whose access keys were revoked or deleted between syncs. Rules that gate on `_ont_active` use `COALESCE(_ont_active, true)`, so NULL keeps unknown-activity users in scope.
statements.query: MATCH (u:AWSUser) SET u._ont_active = CASE WHEN (u.passwordlastused_dt IS NOT NULL) OR EXISTS((u)-[:AWS_ACCESS_KEY]->(:AccountAccessKey {status: 'Active'})) THEN true ELSE NULL END
